println.description: Prints the given message and a newline to standard output

print.description: Prints the given message to standard output without a newline

readLine.description: Reads a line from standard input, returns null if EOF reached

listOf.description: Returns a new read-only list of given elements

mutableListOf.description: Returns a new MutableList with the given elements

mapOf.signature: fun <K, V> mapOf(vararg pairs: Pair<K, V>): Map<K, V>
mapOf.description: Returns a new read-only map with the specified key-value pairs

mutableMapOf.signature: fun <K, V> mutableMapOf(vararg pairs: Pair<K, V>): MutableMap<K, V>
mutableMapOf.description: Returns a new MutableMap with the specified key-value pairs

setOf.description: Returns a new read-only set with the given elements

mutableSetOf.description: Returns a new MutableSet with the given elements

arrayOf.description: Returns an array containing the specified elements

intArrayOf.description: Returns an IntArray containing the specified elements

let.description: Calls the specified function block with this value as its argument and returns its result

run.signature: fun <T, R> T.run(block: T.() -> R): R
run.description: Calls the specified function block with this value as its receiver and returns its result

with.signature: fun <T, R> with(receiver: T, block: T.() -> R): R
with.description: Calls the specified function block with the given receiver as its receiver and returns its result

apply.description: Calls the specified function block with this value as its receiver and returns this value

also.description: Calls the specified function block with this value as its argument and returns this value

takeIf.description: Returns this value if it satisfies the given predicate or null if it doesn't

takeUnless.description: Returns this value if it does not satisfy the given predicate or null if it does

repeat.description: Executes the given function action specified number of times

require.signature: fun require(value: Boolean, lazyMessage: () -> Any): Unit
require.description: Throws an IllegalArgumentException if the value is false

check.signature: fun check(value: Boolean, lazyMessage: () -> Any): Unit
check.description: Throws an IllegalStateException if the value is false

error.description: Throws an IllegalStateException with the given message

TODO.description: Always throws NotImplementedError stating that operation is not implemented

lazy.description: Creates a new instance of Lazy that uses the specified initialization function

sequence.signature: fun <T> sequence(block: suspend SequenceScope<T>.() -> Unit): Sequence<T>
sequence.description: Builds a Sequence lazily yielding values one by one

buildList.description: Builds a new read-only List by populating a MutableList using the given builder action

buildMap.signature: fun <K, V> buildMap(builderAction: MutableMap<K, V>.() -> Unit): Map<K, V>
buildMap.description: Builds a new read-only Map by populating a MutableMap using the given builder action

buildSet.description: Builds a new read-only Set by populating a MutableSet using the given builder action

forEach.description: Performs the given action on each element

map.description: Returns a list containing the results of applying the given transform function to each element

filter.description: Returns a list containing only elements matching the given predicate

flatMap.description: Returns a single list of all elements yielded from results of transform function being invoked on each element

reduce.signature: fun <S, T : S> Iterable<T>.reduce(operation: (acc: S, T) -> S): S
reduce.description: Accumulates value starting with the first element and applying operation from left to right

fold.signature: fun <T, R> Iterable<T>.fold(initial: R, operation: (acc: R, T) -> R): R
fold.description: Accumulates value starting with initial value and applying operation from left to right

groupBy.signature: fun <T, K> Iterable<T>.groupBy(keySelector: (T) -> K): Map<K, List<T>>
groupBy.description: Groups elements of the collection by the key returned by the given function

sortedBy.signature: fun <T, R : Comparable<R>> Iterable<T>.sortedBy(selector: (T) -> R?): List<T>
sortedBy.description: Returns a list of all elements sorted according to natural sort order of the value returned by the selector

associate.signature: fun <T, K, V> Iterable<T>.associate(transform: (T) -> Pair<K, V>): Map<K, V>
associate.description: Returns a Map containing key-value pairs provided by transform function applied to elements

first.description: Returns the first element, throws NoSuchElementException if empty

firstOrNull.description: Returns the first element, or null if the collection is empty

any.description: Returns true if at least one element matches the given predicate

all.description: Returns true if all elements match the given predicate

none.description: Returns true if no elements match the given predicate

count.description: Returns the number of elements in the collection

joinToString.description: Creates a string from all the elements separated using separator

toList.description: Returns a List containing all elements

toMutableList.description: Returns a MutableList filled with all elements of this collection

toSet.description: Returns a Set of all elements

toMap.signature: fun <K, V> Iterable<Pair<K, V>>.toMap(): Map<K, V>
toMap.description: Returns a new map containing all key-value pairs from the given collection of pairs

toInt.description: Parses the string as a signed Int number and returns the result

toDouble.description: Parses the string as a Double number and returns the result

toIntOrNull.description: Parses the string as a signed Int number and returns the result or null if the string is not a valid integer

runBlocking.signature: fun <T> runBlocking(context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext, block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T): T
runBlocking.description: Runs a new coroutine and blocks the current thread until its completion

launch.signature: fun CoroutineScope.launch(context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext, start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT, block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> Unit): Job
launch.description: Launches a new coroutine without blocking the current thread and returns a reference to it as a Job

async.signature: fun <T> CoroutineScope.async(context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext, start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT, block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T): Deferred<T>
async.description: Creates a coroutine and returns its future result as an implementation of Deferred

withContext.signature: suspend fun <T> withContext(context: CoroutineContext, block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T): T
withContext.description: Calls the specified suspending block with a given coroutine context, suspends until it completes, and returns the result

delay.description: Delays coroutine for a given time without blocking a thread

val.description: Declares a read-only (immutable) property or local variable

var.description: Declares a mutable property or local variable

object.description: Declares a singleton object or object expression

when.description: Starts a when expression (replacement for switch)

for.description: For loop that iterates through anything that provides an iterator

while.description: While loop that executes body while condition is true

return.description: Returns from the nearest enclosing function or anonymous function

try.description: Begins a try-catch exception handling block

catch.description: Handles an exception type in a try block

finally.description: Block that executes after try/catch regardless of exception

suspend.description: Marks a function or lambda as suspending (callable from coroutines)

data.description: Marks a class as a data class, auto-generating equals, hashCode, toString, copy

sealed.description: Marks a class or interface as sealed, restricting subclasses to the same package

enum.description: Declares an enum class with a fixed set of instances

override.description: Marks a member override of a superclass member or interface member

open.description: Allows a class to be inherited from or a member to be overridden

abstract.description: Marks a class or member as abstract

companion.description: Declares a companion object inside a class

inline.description: Requests the compiler to inline the function body and lambda parameters at call sites

infix.description: Allows a function to be called with infix notation

operator.description: Marks a function as an operator overload

typealias.description: Provides alternative names for existing types

lateinit.description: Allows initializing a non-null property outside of a constructor body

const.description: Marks a property as a compile-time constant

Double.description: Represents a double-precision 64-bit IEEE 754 floating point number

Float.description: Represents a single-precision 32-bit IEEE 754 floating point number

Boolean.description: Represents a value which is either true or false

String.description: Represents an array of characters. Strings are immutable

Unit.description: The type with only one value: the Unit object. Corresponds to void in Java

Nothing.description: Nothing has no instances. Used as return type of functions that never return

Any.description: The root of the Kotlin class hierarchy. Every Kotlin class has Any as a superclass

List.description: A generic ordered collection of elements that supports indexed access

MutableList.description: A generic ordered collection of elements that supports adding and removing elements

Map.description: A collection that holds pairs of objects (keys and values)

MutableMap.description: A modifiable collection that holds pairs of objects and supports adding and removing entries

Set.description: A generic unordered collection of elements that does not support duplicate elements

MutableSet.description: A generic unordered collection of elements that does not support duplicate elements and supports adding and removing elements

Pair.signature: data class Pair<out A, out B>(val first: A, val second: B)
Pair.description: Represents a generic pair of two values

Triple.signature: data class Triple<out A, out B, out C>(val first: A, val second: B, val third: C)

Sequence.description: A sequence that returns values through its iterator, values are evaluated lazily

Comparable.description: Classes which inherit from this interface have a defined total ordering between their instances

Iterable.description: Classes that inherit from this interface can be represented as a sequence of elements that can be iterated over

Collection.description: A generic collection of elements, provides read-only methods
